title: ulmus × daveyi
intro: Ulmus × daveyi, also known as the hybrid elm, is a cross between the English elm (Ulmus procera) and the Camperdown elm (Ulmus glabra 'Camperdownii'). The medicinal properties of Ulmus × daveyi lie in its bark, which contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins. The health benefits of this plant include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Ulmus × daveyi can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal and therapeutic uses.
